title: hieracium lachenalii subsp. erythropodum
intro: Hieracium lachenalii subsp. erythropodum, also known as the wood hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family Asteraceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and may also help to treat conditions like fever and skin irritations. Hieracium lachenalii subsp. erythropodum can be used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, but its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
